meta.title: Original Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter Practice Question Bank
meta.chapter: NCERT Class 12 Physics Chapter 11 - Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
meta.licenseNote: Original educational questions generated for this project. No copied coaching material or copyrighted question banks.

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Photon Energy?
questions.answer: E = hf
questions.solution: Photon Energy: Energy of one photon. Therefore the correct relation is E = hf.
questions.commonTrap: Intensity is number of photons, not energy per photon.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Photon Energy by Wavelength?
questions.answer: E = hc/lambda
questions.solution: Photon Energy by Wavelength: Shorter wavelength means higher photon energy. Therefore the correct relation is E = hc/lambda.
questions.commonTrap: Use SI or eV-nm conversion carefully.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Work Function?
questions.answer: phi = h f0
questions.solution: Work Function: Minimum removal energy. Therefore the correct relation is phi = h f0.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Threshold Frequency?
questions.answer: f0 = phi/h
questions.solution: Threshold Frequency: Minimum frequency for emission. Therefore the correct relation is f0 = phi/h.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Einstein Photoelectric Equation?
questions.answer: Kmax = hf - phi
questions.solution: Einstein Photoelectric Equation: Energy conservation for photoemission. Therefore the correct relation is Kmax = hf - phi.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Stopping Potential?
questions.answer: eV0 = Kmax
questions.solution: Stopping Potential: Stopping potential measures maximum kinetic energy per charge. Therefore the correct relation is eV0 = Kmax.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Stopping Potential Graph?
questions.answer: V0 = (h/e)f - phi/e
questions.solution: Stopping Potential Graph: Linear graph of stopping potential vs frequency. Therefore the correct relation is V0 = (h/e)f - phi/e.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Photon Momentum?
questions.answer: p = h/lambda
questions.solution: Photon Momentum: Photon carries momentum. Therefore the correct relation is p = h/lambda.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents de Broglie Wavelength?
questions.answer: lambda = h/p
questions.solution: de Broglie Wavelength: Matter wavelength. Therefore the correct relation is lambda = h/p.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Matter Wave for Speed?
questions.answer: lambda = h/(mv)
questions.solution: Matter Wave for Speed: Non-relativistic matter wave. Therefore the correct relation is lambda = h/(mv).
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Electron Wavelength by Voltage?
questions.answer: lambda = h/sqrt(2meV)
questions.solution: Electron Wavelength by Voltage: Wavelength of accelerated electron. Therefore the correct relation is lambda = h/sqrt(2meV).
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Bragg Condition?
questions.answer: 2d sin theta = n lambda
questions.solution: Bragg Condition: Diffraction condition for electron waves. Therefore the correct relation is 2d sin theta = n lambda.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Electronvolt?
questions.solution: Electronvolt: Convenient atomic energy unit. Therefore the correct relation is 1 eV = 1.6 x 10^-19 J.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.

questions.solution: Photon Energy by Wavelength: Shorter wavelength means higher photon energy. Therefore the correct relation is E = hc/lambda.
questions.commonTrap: Use SI or eV-nm conversion carefully.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Work Function?
questions.answer: phi = h f0

questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Threshold Frequency?
questions.answer: f0 = phi/h

questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Einstein Photoelectric Equation?
questions.answer: Kmax = hf - phi
